description: Structural invariants every generated IAM policy must satisfy.

invariants.rule: A statement with Action='*' or '<service>:*' must not pair with Resource='*'. Wildcards on both axes equate to AdministratorAccess.
Statements include an explicit Deny for cross-service privilege escalation actions (iam:PassRole, iam:CreateAccessKey, iam:UpdateAssumeRolePolicy) unless the role legitimately needs them.
Any iam:PassRole grant lists concrete role ARNs under Resource. Resource='*' on PassRole is a known privilege-escalation primitive.
Cross-account trust policies (sts:AssumeRole from a Principal in a different account) include Condition.StringEquals['sts:ExternalId'] with a unique-per-consumer value. Mitigates Confused Deputy.

Any role granting administrative or sensitive actions (iam:*, ec2:Terminate*, rds:Delete*, kms:Decrypt on production keys, secretsmanager:GetSecretValue) requires aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent: true in its trust or permission policy.
MaxSessionDuration is set to ≤ 3600 (1h) for human-assumable roles and ≤ 43200 (12h) for break-glass roles.
Every IAM principal carries tags: owner, environment, purpose. Used by SCPs and cost allocation.
Prefer managed policies for shared permission sets so changes propagate; inline policies are acceptable for one-off, single-principal scopes.
AWS-managed AdministratorAccess (arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess) is not attached to any routine identity; only to a break-glass role with monitoring.
Runtime workload roles (the role a pod / Lambda / EC2 actually assumes) do not include iam:Create*, iam:Update*, iam:Delete*, iam:Put*, sts:* (other than AssumeRole on a narrow set of ARNs).
Sensitive grants are constrained by aws:SourceIp, aws:SourceVpc, or aws:SourceVpce when the workload runs in a known network boundary.
If long-lived access keys exist, the policy/automation rotates them every ≤ 90 days and deactivates keys unused for ≥ 90 days.
